title: Delicate Vietnamese Chicken Com Tam
description: Citrusy, glassy-jelly chicken com tam with buns, toast, and Mediterranean-friendly flavors, perfect for meal prep.

ingredients.name: boneless skinless chicken breast
ingredients.notes: 1 tbsp olive oil, 1 tbsp sesame oil for pan

instructions: Marinate the chicken breast in a mixture of soy sauce, fish sauce, grated ginger, minced garlic, and a pinch of salt for 10 minutes.

Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat and sear the chicken until golden on both sides, about 4 minutes per side, then reduce heat to medium and cook until internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C).
While the chicken cooks, whisk orange juice, lemon juice, and honey in a small bowl until combined; then sprinkle agar-agar over the mixture and whisk until fully dissolved.
Transfer the citrus mixture to a small saucepan and bring to a simmer over low heat, stirring constantly for 3 minutes until it thickens into a glossy jelly.
Remove the chicken from the pan, let it rest for 5 minutes, then slice it thinly.
In a small bowl, combine the cooked rice with a splash of olive oil and a drizzle of sesame oil, tossing to coat.
Divide the rice into two portions, placing each on a split bun or on the slice of toast.
Top each bun or toast with slices of cucumber, diced tomato, and sliced green onions, then add a generous spoonful of the citrus jelly.
Arrange the sliced chicken on top of the jelly, garnishing with chopped cilantro.
Serve immediately or store each portion in an airtight container for up to 3 days in the fridge, reheating the chicken gently before eating.
